info.title: IronAuth Management API
info.description: The OpenAPI-first management API (issue #11). Every list endpoint uses cursor pagination; every POST honors Idempotency-Key; every response carries RateLimit headers; every mutation writes a same-transaction audit row. Credentials are environment-scoped management keys plus a config bootstrap operator token.

get.summary: List operators (cursor paginated).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
The desired page size, a positive integer. Clamped to
`[1, max_page_size]`; defaults to the configured default when absent.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
The opaque cursor from a previous page's `next_cursor`. Absent for the
first page (keyset pagination; there is no offset).

get.summary: Get one operator.
List every resource type and its promotion classification.
List tenants (cursor paginated).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
The desired page size, a positive integer. Clamped to
`[1, max_page_size]`; defaults to the configured default when absent.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
The opaque cursor from a previous page's `next_cursor`. Absent for the
first page (keyset pagination; there is no offset).

post.summary: Create a tenant and its first environment.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Required. Replaying a POST with the same key returns the original response without re-executing.

422.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different request

get.summary: Get one tenant.
List environments under a tenant (cursor paginated).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
The desired page size, a positive integer. Clamped to
`[1, max_page_size]`; defaults to the configured default when absent.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
The opaque cursor from a previous page's `next_cursor`. Absent for the
first page (keyset pagination; there is no offset).

post.summary: Create an environment under a tenant.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Required. Replaying a POST with the same key returns the original response without re-executing.

409.description: Parent tenant is not active (suspended or offboarded)

422.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different request

get.summary:
Get one environment. Reachable by the operator or a management key scoped to
exactly this `(tenant, environment)`.

403.description: Credential presented against the wrong environment or plane

get.summary: Get a dynamically registered client's verification state.

404.description: Not found (absent, not a DCR client, or in another scope)

post.summary: Verify a dynamically registered client, lifting its unverified-client quarantine.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Required. Replaying a POST with the same key returns the original response without re-executing.

404.description: Not found (absent, not a DCR client, or in another scope)

422.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different request

post.summary: Transactionally apply a promotion plan onto the target environment.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Optional. Apply is idempotent by construction: re-applying an already-applied plan is a no-op.

200.description: The plan was applied (the applied diff) or was already applied (a no-op).

400.description: The source snapshot document is invalid

409.description: The target drifted since the plan was computed (a structured drift error); nothing was changed.

422.description: A reference does not resolve in the target environment at apply time; nothing was changed.

post.summary: Dry-run a promotion into the target environment, returning a reviewable plan.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Optional. A plan is a pure dry run and changes nothing, so a replay is inherently safe.

requestBody.description: The source config snapshot document (canonical, secret-free JSON), the same shape the snapshot export returns.

200.description: The reviewable promotion plan: a stable plan id, the target's base and result revisions, the resolved references, and the structured diff (per resource: create, update, or delete with before and after).

400.description: The source snapshot document is invalid

422.description: One or more references do not resolve in the target environment (the plan fails closed)

get.summary: Export the environment's canonical secret-free config snapshot.

200.description: The canonical, deterministic, secret-free config snapshot of the environment's promotable resources (clients, resource servers, DCR policies). The document validates against the published JSON Schema at docs/snapshot/snapshot.schema.json; two exports of the same configuration are byte-identical, and no secret or key material appears in it.

post.summary: Mint a DCR initial access token. Returns the plaintext token ONCE.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Required. Replaying a POST with the same key returns the original response without re-executing.

200.description: Idempotent replay; the token is omitted

201.description: Created; the token is present on this response ONCE

400.description: Malformed request or unknown policy name

422.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different request

get.summary: List DCR policies in an environment (cursor paginated).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
The desired page size, a positive integer. Clamped to
`[1, max_page_size]`; defaults to the configured default when absent.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
The opaque cursor from a previous page's `next_cursor`. Absent for the
first page (keyset pagination; there is no offset).

post.summary: Create a named, reusable DCR policy.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Required. Replaying a POST with the same key returns the original response without re-executing.

409.description: A policy with this name already exists

422.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different request

get.summary: Export every identity of an environment as newline-delimited import records.

200.description: The full identity export as newline-delimited JSON (application/x-ndjson): one import record per line, carrying every user's id, login handle, external id, lifecycle state, standard claims, identity traits and schema version, and the password verifier with its algorithm tag and parameters (a native Argon2id PHC string, or the imported foreign hash for a user not yet logged in). This is the exact format the streaming bulk import consumes, so it re-imports into a fresh instance losslessly with logins intact. Documented at docs/exit-guide.md.

get.summary: List invitations under an environment (cursor paginated), filterable by state.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Only invitations in this lifecycle state.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
The desired page size, a positive integer. Clamped to
`[1, max_page_size]`; defaults to the configured default when absent.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
The opaque cursor from a previous page's `next_cursor`. Absent for the
first page (keyset pagination; there is no offset).

post.summary: Create an invitation for a new identity.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Required. Replaying a POST with the same key returns the original response (WITHOUT the one-time token) without re-executing.

201.description: Created; the one-time token is returned once

409.description: The invited identifier is already in use

422.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different request

get.summary: Get one invitation.

404.description: Not found (absent or in another scope)

post.summary: Resend a pending invitation: invalidate the prior token and issue a fresh one.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Required. Replaying a POST with the same key returns the original response (WITHOUT the fresh token) without re-executing.

200.description: A fresh token was issued; it is returned once

404.description: Not found (absent, not pending, or in another scope)

422.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different request

post.summary: Revoke a pending invitation (its token becomes unredeemable).

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Required. Replaying a POST with the same key returns the original response without re-executing.

404.description: Not found (absent, not pending, or in another scope)

422.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different request

get.summary: List management API keys in an environment (cursor paginated; metadata only).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
The desired page size, a positive integer. Clamped to
`[1, max_page_size]`; defaults to the configured default when absent.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
The opaque cursor from a previous page's `next_cursor`. Absent for the
first page (keyset pagination; there is no offset).

post.summary: Mint a management API key in an environment. Returns the secret ONCE.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Required. Replaying a POST with the same key returns the original response without re-executing.

200.description: Idempotent replay; the secret is omitted and secret_already_issued is true

201.description: Created; the secret is present on this response ONCE

422.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different request

get.summary: Get one management API key's metadata (never its secret).

404.description: Not found (absent or in another scope)

delete.summary: Revoke a management API key (soft delete; idempotent).

404.description: Not found (absent, already revoked, or in another scope)

get.summary: List a scope's migration runs (cursor paginated).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
The desired page size, a positive integer. Clamped to
`[1, max_page_size]`; defaults to the configured default when absent.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
The opaque cursor from a previous page's `next_cursor`. Absent for the
first page (keyset pagination; there is no offset).

200.description: A page of the environment's migration runs

get.summary: Read one migration run: its state, per-state counts, and LIVE invariant evaluations.

200.description: The run's current state, per-state record counts, and the live invariant evaluations (re-derived from the database on every call), with the blocking invariants surfaced. A run cannot complete while any invariant is unsatisfied.

get.summary: Page the specific records violating one of a run's invariants (cursor paginated).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The desired page size, a positive integer.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The opaque cursor from a previous page's `next_cursor`.

parameters.name: invariant
parameters.description:
Which invariant's offending records to enumerate: `count`, `consistency`, or
`backfill_sentinel`. Defaults to `consistency`. The count invariant is a scalar
discrepancy with no enumerable rows, so it returns an empty page.

200.description: A page of the records violating the selected invariant, each naming the offending identity (opened) and its reason

get.summary: Report the environment's lazy-migration progress.

200.description: The environment's lazy-migration progress: the total user count, how many are on the native Argon2id verifier (migrated), how many still carry an imported foreign hash (the straggler tail a #55 bulk import closes out), and this node's circuit-breaker state. The migrated count, hook latency, error/timeout rates, and breaker state are also exported as Prometheus metrics.

post.summary: Verify a user credential for a successor system's lazy migration.

200.description: The verification verdict. On success it carries the stable subject and the user's profile (claims and traits), so the successor migrates the full identity. A wrong password, unknown account, or fenced account returns verified=false with no distinguishing oracle. Enabled and credentialed through environment-scoped config; documented at docs/exit-guide.md.

401.description: Missing or invalid outbound verification token

404.description: The endpoint is disabled, or the environment was not found

parameters.name: limit

parameters.name: cursor

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Required. Replaying a POST with the same key returns the original response without re-executing.

422.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different request

get.summary: Get one organization.

delete.summary: Deactivate an organization (soft delete; idempotent in effect).

404.description: Not found (absent, or already deactivated: a repeat delete)

get.summary:
List the refresh-token families in an environment (cursor paginated), searchable by

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: Only sessions or families of this subject (a `usr_...` id).

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: Only sessions or families of this client (a `cli_...` id).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
The desired page size, a positive integer. Clamped to
`[1, max_page_size]`; defaults to the configured default when absent.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
The opaque cursor from a previous page's `next_cursor`. Absent for the

get.summary: Inspect one refresh-token family.

404.description: Not found (absent or in another scope)

get.summary:
List the sessions in an environment (cursor paginated), searchable by user and by
client.

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: Only sessions or families of this subject (a `usr_...` id).

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: Only sessions or families of this client (a `cli_...` id).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
The desired page size, a positive integer. Clamped to
`[1, max_page_size]`; defaults to the configured default when absent.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
The opaque cursor from a previous page's `next_cursor`. Absent for the

post.summary:
Revoke a BATCH of sessions in one audited transaction. A foreign-scope id in the
batch is a uniform no-op (never a cross-tenant revocation).

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Required. Replaying a POST with the same key returns the original response without re-executing.

400.description: Malformed request or too many sessions

422.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different request

get.summary: Inspect one session, whatever its lifecycle state (live, revoked, or rotated away).

404.description: Not found (absent or in another scope)

post.summary:
Revoke ONE session. It stops resolving immediately and its session-bound refresh
families are revoked with it; the `offline_access` families survive unless
`hard_kill` is set.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Required. Replaying a POST with the same key returns the original response without re-executing.

404.description: Not found (absent or in another scope)

422.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different request

get.summary:
List users under an environment (cursor paginated), filterable by state,
external id, and identifier.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Only users in this lifecycle state.

parameters.name: external_id
parameters.description: Only the user whose external id equals this value.

parameters.name: identifier
parameters.description: Only the user whose login handle equals this value.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
The desired page size, a positive integer. Clamped to
`[1, max_page_size]`; defaults to the configured default when absent.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
The opaque cursor from a previous page's `next_cursor`. Absent for the
first page (keyset pagination; there is no offset).

post.summary: Create a user under an environment.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Required. Replaying a POST with the same key returns the original response without re-executing.

409.description: The id, login handle, or external id is already taken

422.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different request

get.summary: Get one user.

404.description: Not found (absent or in another scope)

delete.summary: Delete a user (a soft-delete offboarding that cascades sessions).

parameters.name: hard_kill
parameters.description: Kill the `offline_access` refresh families too (default false).

404.description: Not found (absent, already deleted, or in another scope)

patch.summary: Update a user's profile (RFC 7396 partial patch of the standard claims).

404.description: Not found (absent or in another scope)

put.summary: Link an external id to a user.

404.description: Not found (absent or in another scope)

409.description: The external id is already claimed by another user

404.description: Not found (absent or in another scope)

post.summary:
Revoke EVERYTHING for one user: every session, cascading to the user's
refresh-token families in the SAME transaction. The `offline_access` families
SURVIVE (issue #21's offline-survives-logout semantic) unless the explicit
`hard_kill` flag is passed, which also revokes their grants so every already-issued
access token dies immediately.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Required. Replaying a POST with the same key returns the original response without re-executing.

200.description: Every session of the user was revoked

404.description: Not found (absent or in another scope)

422.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different request

post.summary: Transition a user's lifecycle state.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Required. Replaying a POST with the same key returns the original response without re-executing.

404.description: Not found (absent or in another scope)

409.description: The transition is not valid from the user's current state

422.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different request

post.summary: Restore a soft-deleted (offboarded) tenant inside its retention window.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Required. Replaying a POST with the same key returns the original response without re-executing.

404.description: Not found (never offboarded, already restored, or purged)

409.description: Retention window elapsed; restore no longer offered

422.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different request

post.summary: Resume a suspended tenant (restore data-plane service; no data loss).

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Required. Replaying a POST with the same key returns the original response without re-executing.

422.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different request

post.summary: Suspend a tenant (fence its data plane; reversible).

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Required. Replaying a POST with the same key returns the original response without re-executing.

422.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different request

ApplyConfigPromotionRequest.description:
The apply request body: the source snapshot to promote, plus the plan's captured
`base_revision` precondition (issue #44).

source.description:
The source config snapshot document (the same canonical, secret-free shape
the snapshot export returns). Validated before anything is applied.

BulkRevocationView.description:
The result of a BULK session revocation (issue #32). States the post-condition; see
[`SessionRevocationView`] for why.

hard_kill.description: Whether the `offline_access` refresh families were killed too.

sessions_targeted.description:
How many of the named sessions were IN SCOPE and therefore targeted. Ids that
were malformed or belonged to another tenant or environment are silently
dropped (scope fence), so this can be lower than the number of ids sent.

BulkRevokeSessionsRequest.description: The body of a BULK session revocation (issue #32).

hard_kill.description:
Also revoke the `offline_access` families and their grants (a HARD KILL). The
default preserves them; see [`RevokeSessionsRequest::hard_kill`].

session_ids.description:
The sessions to revoke. Every id is scope-FENCED: one belonging to another
tenant or environment is a uniform no-op (never an error that would confirm
its existence), so a batch can never reach across a scope boundary.

ClientVerificationView.description:
A dynamically registered client's verification state (issue #31), as returned by
the management API. `quarantined` is the live gate the authorization/consent path
honors; `verified_at_unix_ms` records when an admin lifted the quarantine.

quarantined.description: Whether the client is under the unverified-client quarantine.

verified.description: Whether an admin has verified the client (the quarantine is lifted).

verified_at_unix_ms.description:
When the client was verified, milliseconds since the Unix epoch, or null if
never verified.

CreateDcrPolicyRequest.description:
The body to create a named, reusable DCR policy (issue #31).

`primitives` is the ordered list of policy primitives, each a JSON object with a
`kind` of `force`, `restrict`, `reject`, or `default` plus its fields (a `force`
or `default` carries `property` and `value`; a `restrict` carries `property` and
`allowed`; a `reject` carries `property`). The management API validates the shape
at create time against the OIDC policy engine.

FOOTGUN: `restrict` only narrows a property that is PRESENT; an OMITTED property is
unconstrained by it, and the endpoint then applies the spec default for the omitted
property. So a `restrict` whose `allowed` set excludes the spec default can be
dodged by simply omitting the property (the client ends up with the default the
restrict meant to forbid). To make a property mandatory and pinned, pair the
`restrict` with a `default` (fills the omitted value so the restrict validates a
present one) or a `force` (overrides it outright). This holds at registration AND
at RFC 7592 update time.

name.description: The policy name, unique per environment (referenced by name at token mint).

primitives.description: The ordered primitive list (force / restrict / reject / default objects).

CreateEnvironmentRequest.description: The body to create an environment under a tenant.

custom_domain.description:
The custom domain to configure. Required when `kind` is `prod` (the
production custom-domain guardrail); optional otherwise.

kind.description:
The environment kind: `dev`, `staging`, or `prod` (issue #42). Required;
an unknown value is rejected. The kind fixes the environment's guardrail
class (dev and staging inherit the relaxed non-production set; prod gets
the hard production set).

region.description:
The environment's data-residency region pin (issue #46). When present it must
be one of the operator's configured regions (the same set the tenant
`home_region` validates against), is persisted immutably, and appears in every
read; when omitted the environment records no region. Nothing routes or
replicates by it yet.

CreateInitialAccessTokenRequest.description: The body to mint a DCR initial access token (RFC 7591, issue #31).

expires_in_secs.description: The token lifetime in seconds from now (from the server clock).

max_uses.description:
The maximum number of registrations this token may authorize, or null for
unlimited (within its lifetime).

policy_names.description:
The ordered names of the policies to attach as this token's chain. Each must
name a policy that exists in this environment; the chain is resolved to its
primitives and snapshotted onto the token, so a later edit of a named policy
never changes an already-minted token. Empty means an unconstrained token.

CreateInvitationRequest.description:
The body to create an invitation (issue #60). The invited `identifier` is
required; every other field has a safe default.

oneOf.description:
The OPTIONAL primary-login credential the invitee enrolls on accept (default
`password`). A `passkey` invitation provisions no password.

expires_in_secs.description:
The OPTIONAL token lifetime in seconds (default: the configured invitation
TTL). Bounds how long the invite link stays acceptable.

identifier.description:
The invited identifier (an email or other login handle), unique per scope: a
`pending_verification` user is provisioned for it. An identifier already in use
by an existing user returns 409.

org_context.description:
An OPTIONAL opaque org handle M10 layers membership semantics on. Carried,
not interpreted here.

CreateManagementKeyRequest.description: The body to mint a management API key in a `(tenant, environment)` scope.

CreateOrganizationRequest.description: The body to create an organization in an environment.

CreateTenantRequest.description: The body to create a tenant. The first environment is created with it.

environment_custom_domain.description:
The first environment's custom domain, if any. Required only when
`environment_kind` is `prod` (the production custom-domain guardrail).

environment_display_name.description: The first environment's display name. Defaults to `development`.

environment_kind.description:
The first environment's kind: `dev`, `staging`, or `prod` (issue #42).
Defaults to `dev`, the relaxed non-production kind that requires no custom
domain, so a tenant is always creatable in one call with sane defaults.

home_region.description:
The tenant's data-residency region (issue #46). When present it must be one
of the operator's configured regions, is persisted immutably, and appears in
every read; when omitted the tenant records no region. Nothing routes or
replicates by it yet.

CreateUserRequest.description: The body to create a user (issue #52). Every field but `identifier` is optional.

claims.description: An OPTIONAL standard-claim JSON document (issue #15), stored verbatim.

external_id.description: An OPTIONAL external correlation id to link at creation (unique per scope).

id.description:
An OPTIONAL caller-supplied user id (`usr_...`, in this scope). A supplied id
already taken in the scope returns 409; absent, a fresh id is minted.

identifier.description: The login handle, unique per scope.

password_hash.description:
An OPTIONAL precomputed Argon2id PHC verifier string. Absent, the user is
created without a usable credential and cannot log in until one is set.

oneOf.description:
The OPTIONAL initial lifecycle state (default `active`). Must be a creatable
state (not `scheduled_offboarding`, which needs a timestamp).

items.description: The policies on this page, oldest first.

next_cursor.description: The opaque cursor for the next page, or null if this is the last page.

DcrPolicyView.description: A DCR policy, as returned by the management API.

created_at_unix_ms.description: Creation time, milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

items.description: The environments on this page, oldest first.

next_cursor.description: The opaque cursor for the next page, or null if this is the last page.

EnvironmentView.description: An environment, as returned by the management API.

created_at_unix_ms.description: Creation time, milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

custom_domain.description:
The configured custom domain, if any. A production environment always has
one (the custom-domain guardrail); a non-production environment may omit it.

guardrail_class.description: The guardrail class the kind maps onto: `non-production` or `production`.

guardrails.description: The typed guardrails this environment enforces, derived from its kind.

region.description:
The recorded per-environment data-residency region pin (issue #46), or null
when the environment pins no region. Immutable after create; nothing routes
by it yet.

tenant_id.description: The tenant the environment belongs to (`ten_...`).

ErrorBody.description:
The structured error body every management error renders to. Stable shape, so
generated SDKs can type it from the first endpoint.

actual_scope.description: Present only on a wrong-scope error: the scope the request targeted.

error.description:
A stable, machine-readable error type (for example `not_found`,
`wrong_scope`, `idempotency_key_conflict`).

expected_scope.description:
Present only on a wrong-scope error: the scope the credential is
authorized for.

failed_guardrails.description:
Present only on a guardrail-violation error (issue #42): the stable code of
every guardrail the request failed, so the caller learns each failure at
once (for example `["custom_domain_required", "https_only_redirect_uris"]`).

message.description:
A human-readable message. Never carries a secret; for a wrong-scope error
it names only the scope identifiers the caller already presented.

GuardrailView.description:
The typed guardrails an environment enforces (issue #42), derived purely from
its kind so the production asymmetry can never drift.

allow_insecure_redirect_uris.description:
Whether an `http` loopback redirect URI is registrable (true for
non-production, false for production).

hosted_pages_noindex.description: Whether hosted pages carry a `noindex` marker (true for non-production only).

one_time_view_secrets.description: Whether secret values are one-time-view (true for production only).

require_custom_domain.description: Whether a configured custom domain is required (true for production only).

require_https_redirect_uris.description: Whether every redirect URI must be `https` (true for production only).

show_environment_banner.description: Whether a visible environment banner is shown (true for non-production only).

InitialAccessTokenCreated.description:
The result of minting a DCR initial access token.

On the genuine first creation (HTTP 201) `token` carries the plaintext bearer
token, shown exactly ONCE and never stored. An idempotent replay (HTTP 200) omits
it and sets `token_already_issued`.

`Debug` is hand-written to redact the token so a live credential never reaches a
log line through `{value:?}`.

created_at_unix_ms.description: Creation time, milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

expires_at_unix_ms.description: Expiry time, milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

id.description: The token identifier (`iat_...`; embeds its scope; safe to display).

max_uses.description: The usage limit, or null for unlimited.

token.description:
The plaintext bearer token, present ONLY on the first creation (HTTP 201) and
never stored. Present it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` at registration.

token_already_issued.description:
True on an idempotent replay, when the token has already been issued and is
not repeated.

current_value.description: An operator-safe description of the invariant's current values (no PII).

offending_count.description: The number of records currently violating this invariant.

satisfied.description: Whether the invariant is currently satisfied.

InvitationCreatedView.description:
The result of creating (or resending) an invitation (issue #60): the durable
invitation, PLUS the raw single-use token returned exactly ONCE for out-of-band
delivery. The token is NEVER readable again and is never persisted (only its
digest is stored), so an idempotent replay of the POST returns the invitation
WITHOUT the token.

token.description:
The raw `ira_inv_...` single-use token, returned ONCE at creation/resend for
out-of-band delivery to the invitee. Absent on an idempotent replay (the
token is shown only at the original creation). Compose the accept link by
presenting this token to the public invitation-accept endpoint.

InvitationCredentialTypeView.description:
The primary-login credential an invitation enrolls on accept (issue #60): a
password (the #20 Argon2id path) or a passkey deep link (the Zitadel enrollment
pattern). The stable, closed wire enum the management API exposes.

items.description: The invitations on this page, oldest first.

next_cursor.description: The opaque cursor for the next page, or null if this is the last page.

InvitationStateChangeView.description:
An invitation's lifecycle state after a revoke (issue #60): the deterministic
post-condition, so an Idempotency-Key replay is byte-identical.

state.description: The state the invitation is now in.

InvitationStateView.description:
An invitation's lifecycle state on the wire (issue #60): pending until it is
redeemed (accepted) or invalidated (revoked). Both terminal states make the
token unredeemable.

InvitationView.description:
An invitation, as returned by the management API (issue #60). This is the
DURABLE representation: it NEVER carries the token or its digest (the raw token
is delivered ONCE at create/resend and only its digest is ever stored, so a
database dump yields nothing replayable). The invited identifier is decrypted
from its sealed column for display.

accepted_at_unix_ms.description: When the invitation was redeemed, present only in the accepted state.

created_at_unix_ms.description: Creation time, milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

credential_type.description: The primary-login credential the invitee enrolls on accept.

environment_id.description: The environment the invitation lives in (`env_...`).

expires_at_unix_ms.description: When the token expires, milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

id.description: The invitation identifier (`inv_...`, embeds its scope).

org_context.description: The opaque org handle M10 layers membership on, or null when none.

revoked_at_unix_ms.description: When the invitation was revoked, present only in the revoked state.

target_identifier.description: The invited identifier (an email or login handle), decrypted for display.

tenant_id.description: The tenant the invitation belongs to (`ten_...`).

updated_at_unix_ms.description: Last-mutation time, milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

user_id.description:
The `pending_verification` user (`usr_...`) this invitation provisions and
activates on accept.

LinkExternalIdRequest.description: The body to link an external id to a user (issue #52).

external_id.description: The external correlation id from the tenant's own systems (unique per scope).

ManagementKeyCreated.description:
The result of minting a management API key.

On the genuine first creation (HTTP 201) `secret` carries the full bearer
token, shown exactly ONCE, and `secret_already_issued` is false. The secret
is never stored, so an idempotent replay of the same POST (HTTP 200) returns
this same view with `secret` OMITTED and `secret_already_issued` true. Store
the secret on first receipt; it is never retrievable again.

`Debug` is hand-written to redact the secret so a live token can never reach
a log line through `{value:?}`.

created_at_unix_ms.description: Creation time, milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

secret.description:
The full bearer token, present ONLY on the first creation (HTTP 201) and
never stored. Present it as `Authorization: Bearer <secret>`. Absent on an
idempotent replay (HTTP 200); see `secret_already_issued`. Never
retrievable again.

secret_already_issued.description:
True on an idempotent replay, when the secret has already been issued and
is not repeated. False on the first creation.

ManagementKeyList.description: A page of management API keys.

items.description: The keys on this page, oldest first.

next_cursor.description: The opaque cursor for the next page, or null if this is the last page.

ManagementKeyView.description: A management API key's metadata (never its secret), as returned on read.

created_at_unix_ms.description: Creation time, milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

id.description: The key identifier (`mak_...`, embeds its scope; safe to display).

breaker_state.description:
This node's lazy-migration circuit-breaker state (`closed`, `open`, or
`half_open`), or null when no hook is installed on the node serving this request.

foreign_hash_remaining.description:
How many users still carry an imported foreign password hash (the #55 import tail
awaiting a first-login rehash). A standard #55 bulk import closes this out, after
which the hook can be disabled for the environment.

migrated_users.description:
How many users are on the native Argon2id verifier (migrated): the total minus the
foreign-hash stragglers.

total_users.description: The total number of live users in the environment.

MigrationRunCountsView.description: The per-state record counts of a run (issue #59), re-derived live.

accounted.description: The total accounted records (the sum of the three buckets).

MigrationRunDetailView.description:
One run's full operator view (issue #59): its state, per-state counts, and the LIVE
invariant evaluations, with the blocking invariants surfaced.

abandoned_reason.description: The operator-safe abandonment reason, when abandoned.

backfill_expected.description: The number of records a backfill must mark.

blocking.description:
The names of the invariants currently BLOCKING completion (empty when the run
could complete).

source_total.description: The declared ground-truth source record count.

subject_ref.description: The non-PII link back to the wrapped job, when set.

next_cursor.description: The opaque cursor for the next page, or null on the last page.

MigrationRunSummaryView.description: One run in the paginated list view (issue #59).

backfill_expected.description: The number of records a backfill must mark.

source_total.description: The declared ground-truth source record count.

subject_ref.description: The non-PII link back to the wrapped job, when set.

MigrationRunViolationList.description: A page of the records violating one invariant (issue #59).

items.description: The offending records on this page.

next_cursor.description: The opaque cursor for the next page, or null on the last page.

backfilled.description: Whether a backfill has marked this record.

consistent.description: Whether the identity is in a consistent state.

subject.description: The record's natural subject, opened from its sealed value.

items.description: The operators on this page, oldest first.

next_cursor.description: The opaque cursor for the next page, or null if this is the last page.

OperatorView.description:
An operator, as returned by the management API. The operator plane is the root
of the four-level model; its identifier embeds neither a tenant nor an
environment.

created_at_unix_ms.description: Creation time, milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

items.description: The organizations on this page, oldest first.

next_cursor.description: The opaque cursor for the next page, or null if this is the last page.

OrganizationView.description:
An organization, as returned by the management API. Organizations live inside
environments, so the identifier embeds both the tenant and the environment.

active.description:
Whether the organization is active. Always true on a read (a deactivated
organization reads as not-found); present so the wire shape carries the
active-state field the resource model declares.

created_at_unix_ms.description: Creation time, milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

environment_id.description: The environment the organization lives in (`env_...`).

id.description: The organization identifier (`org_...`, embeds its scope).

tenant_id.description: The tenant the organization belongs to (`ten_...`).

next_cursor.description: The cursor for the next page, or null when this is the last page.

RefreshFamilyView.description:
A refresh-token family, as the fleet-operations surface reports it (issue #32).
Families are searchable fleet resources alongside sessions, so an operator can see
exactly which long-lived credential chains a user or a client holds.

absolute_expires_at_unix_ms.description: The absolute hard cap on the family's rotated lifetime.

client_id.description: The OAuth client the family belongs to.

created_at_unix_ms.description: Creation time, milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

offline.description:
Whether this is an `offline_access` family. An offline family SURVIVES a
logout and a session revocation (issue #21); only an explicit hard kill ends
it.

revoked_at_unix_ms.description: When the family was revoked, milliseconds since the Unix epoch, or null.

scope.description: The granted OAuth scope the family was issued against.

session_ref.description: The authenticating SSO session (`ses_...`), when a session backed the grant.

subject.description: The authenticated end-user subject the family's tokens are minted for.

ResourceTypeView.description:
One resource type's promotion classification, as served by the resource-model
metadata endpoint. This is the machine-readable classification the snapshot
export (5.3) and the promotion engine (5.4) consume so they never maintain a
parallel promotable/runtime list.

level.description:
The scope level its identifier is defined at (`operator`, `tenant`, or
`environment`).

name.description: The resource type's stable wire name (for example `organization`).

items.description: Every first-class resource type and its classification.

RevokeSessionsRequest.description:
The body of a single-session or revoke-everything-for-a-user revocation (issue
#32). Both fields are optional, so an empty body is a plain, offline-preserving
revoke.

hard_kill.description:
Also revoke the user's `offline_access` refresh families AND their grants, so
every already-issued access token dies immediately (a HARD KILL).

The default (`false`) PRESERVES the `offline_access` families, which is the
documented offline-survives-logout semantic (issue #21): a background job
holding an offline token keeps working when the user's browser session is
revoked. Set this only when the intent is to cut a compromised principal off
from everything.

next_cursor.description: The cursor for the next page, or null when this is the last page.

SessionRevocationView.description:
The result of revoking one session (issue #32).

Every revocation view states the POST-CONDITION (what is true now), never a delta
(how many rows this particular call happened to flip). That is deliberate, and it
is what makes the two cross-cutting contracts hold at once:

- **Idempotency-Key replay.** The stored response is written in the SAME
  transaction as the revocation, so its body must be known before the write. A
  post-condition is; a row count is not.
- **The anti-oracle.** An absent session, a session in ANOTHER tenant, and an
  already-revoked session all produce the identical response, so the surface never
  confirms which sessions exist.

The actual cascade is observable where it belongs: the refresh-family fleet list,
which shows exactly which families were revoked and which `offline_access`
families survived.

hard_kill.description:
Whether the `offline_access` refresh families were killed too. When false (the
default) they SURVIVE, which is issue #21's offline-survives-logout semantic.

revoked.description:
Always true: after this call the session does not resolve, whether it was live,
already revoked, or absent (the anti-oracle).

SessionView.description:
A session, as the fleet-operations surface reports it (issue #32).

Sessions are first-class, searchable, metadata-carrying fleet resources rather
than an opaque internal table. The view deliberately reports REVOKED, ROTATED, and
ENDED sessions too (not just live ones), so an operator can inspect the whole
lifecycle: `ended_at` plus `end_cause` say when and why, and `superseded_by` names
the successor when the session was rotated away at a privilege transition.

`user_agent` and `peer_ip` are present only when the operator enabled the
corresponding OFF-BY-DEFAULT binding knob, so the safe default records neither.

absolute_expires_at_unix_ms.description: The absolute hard-cap expiry, milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

auth_methods.description: The recorded authentication methods (space-separated RFC 8176 values).

created_at_unix_ms.description: Creation time, milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

end_cause.description:
Why the session ended (`revoked`, `bulk_revoked`, `user_revoked_all`,
`logged_out`, or `rotated`), or null if it is live.

ended_at_unix_ms.description: When the session ended (revoked or rotated away), or null if it is live.

idle_expires_at_unix_ms.description: The idle timeout, milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

last_seen_at_unix_ms.description: When the session was last seen, milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

peer_ip.description: The recorded peer IP (only when the peer-IP binding knob is on).

revoked_at_unix_ms.description: When the session was revoked, milliseconds since the Unix epoch, or null.

superseded_by.description:
The successor session id when this one was ROTATED away, or null. Its presence
is what distinguishes a rotation from a terminal end.

user_agent.description: The recorded user agent (only when the device binding knob is on).

SetUserStateRequest.description: The body to transition a user's lifecycle state (issue #52).

hard_kill.description:
Whether a session-ending transition also kills the user's `offline_access`
refresh families (default false: they survive).

scheduled_offboarding_at_unix_ms.description:
Required for and only for `scheduled_offboarding`: the instant the worker
executes the offboarding, in milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

TenantCreated.description: The result of creating a tenant: the tenant and its first environment.

items.description: The tenants on this page, oldest first.

next_cursor.description: The opaque cursor for the next page, or null if this is the last page.

TenantStatusView.description:
The result of a tenant lifecycle transition (issue #46): the tenant id and its
new status. It states the POST-CONDITION (what is true after the call), so the
body is known before the write and stored verbatim for an Idempotency-Key
replay, exactly like the session-revocation views.

status.description: The tenant's status after the transition: `active` or `suspended`.

TenantView.description: A tenant, as returned by the management API.

created_at_unix_ms.description: Creation time, milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

home_region.description:
The recorded data-residency region (issue #46), or null when the deployment
pins no region. Immutable after create; nothing routes by it yet.

status.description:
The lifecycle status: `active` or `suspended` (issue #46). A suspended
tenant is fenced off the data plane but keeps all its data and stays visible
here.

claims.description:
The replacement standard-claim JSON document. Absent leaves the claims
unchanged.

UserExternalIdView.description: A user's external id after a link or unlink (issue #52).

external_id.description: The linked external id, or null after an unlink.

id.description: The user the external id belongs to.

items.description: The users on this page, oldest first.

next_cursor.description: The opaque cursor for the next page, or null if this is the last page.

UserRevocationView.description:
The result of revoking every session of one user (issue #32). States the
post-condition; see [`SessionRevocationView`] for why.

hard_kill.description:
Whether the user's `offline_access` refresh families were killed too. When
false (the default) they SURVIVE the mass logout.

revoked.description: Always true: after this call none of the user's sessions resolve.

UserStateChangeView.description:
A user's lifecycle state after a transition (issue #52). The deterministic
post-condition returned by the state and delete operations.

hard_kill.description: Whether the transition killed the user's `offline_access` refresh families.

state.description: The state the user is now in.

UserStateView.description:
A user's lifecycle state on the wire (issue #52). The stable, closed enum the
management API exposes: state changes are explicit API operations validated
against a state machine in the store.

UserView.description:
A user, as returned by the management API (issue #52). The identifier embeds its
tenant and environment. NEVER carries the password hash (a management response
must not return a stored credential, the #11 secret lesson).

created_at_unix_ms.description: Creation time, milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

environment_id.description: The environment the user lives in (`env_...`).

external_id.description:
The external correlation id (decrypted for display), or null when none is
linked.

id.description: The user identifier (`usr_...`, embeds its scope).

scheduled_offboarding_at_unix_ms.description:
The scheduled-offboarding instant (milliseconds since the Unix epoch),
present only in the scheduled-offboarding state.

tenant_id.description: The tenant the user belongs to (`ten_...`).

updated_at_unix_ms.description: Last-mutation time, milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

VerifyCredentialRequest.description:
A successor system's credential-verification request (issue #58): a user's login
handle and the candidate password to check. Neither is ever logged; the password
is compared only against the stored one-way verifier. No `Debug` is derived so a
stray struct dump cannot spill the password.

password.description: The candidate password (never logged or echoed).

VerifyCredentialResponse.description:
The verdict returned to the successor (issue #58): whether the credential is
valid and, on success, the stable subject and the user's exportable profile. No
`Debug` is derived so the profile (claims and traits are PII) cannot spill.

oneOf.description:
The user's profile, present only on a successful verification, so the
successor can seed its own record.

subject.description:
The stable pseudonymous subject (the `usr_` id) of the verified user, present
only when `verified` is true.

verified.description:
Whether the identifier plus password verified against the stored credential
AND the account is permitted to authenticate. A wrong password, an unknown
account, and a fenced account (blocked, disabled, pending verification) all
return `false`, with no oracle distinguishing them.

VerifyProfile.description:
The optional profile returned alongside a successful verification (issue #58):
the user's standard claims and identity traits, so the successor migrates the
full identity, not merely the credential.

claims.description: The user's OIDC standard-claim document, or null when the user has none.

traits.description: The user's identity-traits document, or null when the user has none.

bearer.description: A management key token (mak_...) or the bootstrap operator token.

tags.name: operators
tags.description: Operator plane: the root of the four-level resource model, a read surface above tenants

tags.name: organizations
tags.description: Organization CRUD under an environment: the minimal per-environment shell (M10 adds membership)

tags.name: resource-model
tags.description: The resource-type classification catalog (promotable, runtime, environment-identity)

tags.name: config-promotion
tags.description: Canonical secret-free config snapshot export: the diffable, committable substrate the config-promotion flagship consumes

tags.name: dcr
tags.description: Dynamic Client Registration abuse controls: policies, initial access tokens, client verification

tags.name: sessions
tags.description: Session and refresh-family fleet operations: search, inspect, revoke (single, bulk, and everything-for-a-user with a token-family cascade)

tags.name: users
tags.description: Admin user CRUD, lifecycle state transitions, and external-id correlation, with a session cascade on the session-ending transitions

tags.name: invitations
tags.description: Admin user-invitation CRUD: create (provisioning a pending-verification user and a single-use, expiring, hashed-at-rest token), list, get, revoke, and resend

tags.name: exit
tags.description: The exit-friendliness covenant (issue #58): the full identity export (users, traits, states, external ids, and password hashes with their algorithm tags) in the line-delimited import format, plus the outbound lazy-migration credential-verification endpoint a successor system calls to migrate away

tags.name: migration
tags.description: Inbound lazy-migration progress (issue #56): how far an environment's lazy migration has come and the node's circuit-breaker state
